page_content:
HIST371 Jews and Judaism in Antiquity II: First through Seventh Century (3 Credits) Political, social, and religious history of the Jews from the destruction of the Jerusalem Temple in 70 CE to the Muslim conquests. Special attention to the political transformations in Judaism under late Roman Christianity, and the rise of the Rabbinic movement. Recommended: HIST370. Cross-listed with: JWST326. Credit Only Granted for: HIST371 or JWST326.

HIST376 History of Modern Israel (3 Credits) History of modern Israel since the beginning of the Zionist settlement in 1882. Attention to different interpretations and narratives of Israel's history, including the historical and ideological roots of Zionism, the establishment of the State of Israel, ideological forces, wars, and the triumphs and crises of democracy.Cross-listed with ISRL342. Credit Only Granted for: HIST376 or ISRL342.
